summary: Excellent soundboard stereo mix captures a stellar second set with standout performances of He’s Gone, The Other One, and a classic flow of Bertha into Get Smiles and more.
review: The recording is an excellent stereo sound‑board capture that presents the band in crisp, well‑balanced detail.  Musically the show shines – the second set rolls from a rousing Bertha > Get Smiles into superb renditions of Brokedown Palace, El Paso, He’s Gone and a soaring The Other One, all highlighted by Phil’s bass solo and Keith’s tasteful synth textures.  Reviewers uniformly praise both the performance energy and the high‑quality mix.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are highly positive (4–5 stars) and emphasize an exceptional performance plus a superb stereo soundboard recording, yielding a top‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Stereo mix described as excellent and superb with clear dynamics; no complaints about dropouts or muffiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is praised for its seamless transitions – starting with a rousing Bertha > Get Smiles, moving through jazzy explorations, and building to high‑energy peaks on He’s Gone and The Other One before closing with classic finishes.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note his ferocious leads, tasteful teasing (St. Stephen tease) and overall stellar guitar work throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass solo at the start of The Other One is highlighted as a standout moment; also praised for leading El Paso and creating “dust‑cloud” chord textures.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned by name (Bobby) as part of the top‑line performance, contributing vocals on several songs.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s synth/keyboard work is described as low in the mix but appropriate, adding spacey texture to The Other One; also noted for backing vocals with Donna on a few tunes.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (drummer) receives praise for “funky drums” that drive The Other One and other jams.
